_generated: by tools/gen-manifest.py from templates/*/*_meta.yaml; do not edit by hand

bundles.sizing_guidance: Use lean for a handful of criteria on one story; use full for a complex feature with many scenarios, edge cases, and quality attributes.
bundles.summary: Defines the conditions a user story must satisfy to be accepted, so "done" is verifiable and shared rather than left to interpretation.
bundles.title: Acceptance Criteria

bundles.sizing_guidance: Use lean for a straightforward decision; use full for an architecturally significant one with several options weighed and detailed consequences.
bundles.summary: Records one architecturally significant decision: the forces that made it necessary, the options genuinely considered, the option chosen, and the consequences accepted. Immutable once accepted; superseded by a new record rather than edited.
bundles.title: Architecture Decision Record

bundles.sizing_guidance: Use lean for a single feature or a one-pager; use full for a substantial product area that needs detailed scope, success metrics, and a rollout plan.
bundles.summary: Defines what to build, for whom, and why, with scope and success criteria, so a team can design and build the right thing.
bundles.title: Product Requirements Document

bundles.sizing_guidance: Use lean for a routine release; use full for a major launch with breaking changes, migration steps, and audience-specific sections.
bundles.summary: Communicates what changed in a release to users and stakeholders, so people know what is new, what to do, and what to watch for.
bundles.title: Release Notes

bundles.sizing_guidance: Use lean for a focused, low-blast-radius proposal; use full for a significant decision that needs goals and non-goals, detailed design, alternatives, and a rollout plan.
bundles.summary: Proposes a change and gathers input before the decision is made: the motivation, the proposal, the alternatives, the trade-offs, the open questions, and a place to record the outcome. The pre-decision counterpart to the ADR, which records a decision after it is made.
bundles.title: Request for Comments

bundles.sizing_guidance: Use lean, the Google-style five-section mini design doc, for most changes worth a design doc at all; use full when the design is hard to reverse, crosses teams, or has real non-functional targets, which adds the structural, runtime, interface, and deployment views plus quality attributes and risks.
bundles.summary: Describes how a system will be built: the technical design, its structure, the alternatives weighed, and the cross-cutting concerns, so the design can be reviewed before the code exists. Describes an implementation, as distinct from an RFC (which proposes a decision) and an ADR (which records one).
bundles.title: Software Design Document

bundles.sizing_guidance: Use lean for a single, well-understood story; use full when the story needs detailed context, edge cases, and explicit non-functional notes.
bundles.summary: Describes a unit of work from the user's perspective ("as a [user], I want [goal], so that [benefit]") so a team can build and verify the right thing.
bundles.title: User Story
